offer.what: Residential and commercial solar installation, solar panel maintenance, HVAC, roofing, and windows services
offer.coreOutcome: Lower electricity bills and clean energy independence — homeowners eliminate or dramatically cut utility costs while qualifying for the 30% federal tax credit
offer.pricePoint: High-ticket, custom-quoted (residential solar typically $15k–$30k+); one-time installation fee plus optional maintenance
offer.commitment: One-time installation project (weeks to complete); recurring maintenance contracts available
leadMagnets: Energy incentives guide (30% federal tax credit information)
Free quote / site assessment (implied by solar industry standard)

audience.icp: California homeowners in upper-middle to affluent income brackets (Beverly Hills / greater LA area), ages 35–65, environmentally conscious and financially motivated by long-term ROI on energy bills
painPoints: Among the highest electricity rates in the US — California utility bills are a constant frustration
Confusion and overwhelm around the solar buying process (permits, financing, equipment choices)
Distrust of solar installers — fear of poor installation quality or hidden costs
Not knowing how to navigate federal and state tax incentives before deadlines
Uncertainty about ROI timeline and whether solar is worth it for their specific home
segments: Residential homeowners (primary) — single-family homes seeking energy savings
Commercial property owners / small businesses — cost reduction and sustainability goals
Whole-home improvement buyers — interested in bundled solar + roofing + HVAC + windows packages

salesCycle.length: Weeks to months — requires site assessment, custom proposal, financing/incentive navigation, permits, then installation
salesCycle.trial: Free site assessment / quote (standard in solar industry; explicitly referenced on homepage)
salesCycle.closeMechanism: Sales rep outreach → site assessment → custom proposal → financing options → signed contract
salesCycle.topObjection: High upfront cost and uncertainty about ROI timeline / payback period

ahaMoment: First utility bill after system activation showing dramatic savings (or net-zero / net-positive generation) — typically 30–90 days post-installation

retention.recurring: Annual / periodic solar maintenance contracts; system monitoring and performance check-ups
retention.expansion: Upsell from solar-only to bundled services: HVAC replacement, roofing, windows — whole-home efficiency upgrade
retention.churnRisk: System underperformance (shading, panel degradation), slow post-install support, or customer going elsewhere for maintenance

lifecycleGaps: Welcome & Onboarding — no apparent sequence to guide new leads from inquiry to site assessment
Lead-Magnet Nurture — energy incentives guide likely not followed up with an email sequence
Trial/Consult Conversion — no evident email journey to convert free-quote recipients into signed contracts
Booking, Reminders & No-Show — site assessment appointments likely managed manually with no automated reminders
Value Newsletter — no content marketing email program evident (educational content on solar ROI, incentives, etc.)
Renewal, Anniversary & Expansion — no lifecycle trigger emails to existing customers for maintenance upsells or referral asks
Win-Back / Re-Engagement — no apparent re-engagement flow for cold leads or past quote recipients
toneVoice: Professional yet approachable; forward-thinking and values-driven; emphasizes sustainability and community impact; confident without being salesy — positions as a trusted partner rather than a vendor
compliance: California contractor license CSLB# 1025901; standard CAN-SPAM compliance required; no HIPAA or financial regulation exposure; solar incentive claims must be accurate and current (federal tax credit percentages/deadlines)
